text:
Black Infant Health Program | Kern County, CA
Welcome to the Black Infant Health (BIH) Program
The Black Infant Health (BIH) Program is dedicated to empowering Black/African American women by providing free services that promote healthy pregnancies and strong babies. Our supportive, culturally centered approach connects you with the care, resources, and sisterhood you deserve.
Why Enroll in BIH?
Culturally Affirming Support:
Engage in group sessions with other Black women who understand your journey.
Expert Guidance:
Connect with Family Health Advocates, Public Health Nurses, and Mental Health Workers.
Healthy Pregnancy & Beyond:
Learn life skills, stress reduction techniques, and strategies for overall well-being.
Resources & Referrals:
Gain access to valuable community resources for you and your baby.
Enroll Today
Who Can Join?
Black women who are 16 years or older
Pregnant or up to six months postpartum at the time of enrollment
Open to all income levels—services are free!
How to Enroll
If you would like to refer yourself or another person to BIH, please call
(661) 321-3000
. You may also submit a
referral form
by fax to
(661) 868-1291
or by e-mail to
bih@kerncounty.com
.
